$comment: Intiface Central user device configuration for SlopDrive-32.
This teaches Intiface to recognize the machine as a TCode v0.3 linear device
over Bluetooth LE, WebSocket, and USB Serial - all three transports the
firmware supports. The on-device parser is identical for every transport, so
one protocol definition (tcode-v03) covers them all.
HOW TO USE:
1. Open Intiface Central -> Settings -> 'Device Config File'
(older builds: the gear/Advanced page has a 'User Device Config' picker).
2. Point it at THIS file. Restart the Intiface engine if prompted.
3. Start scanning:
- Bluetooth: pick Bluetooth in the machine's web UI (Settings ->
Connection), then Start Scanning in Intiface. 'SlopDrive-32' appears.
- WebSocket / Serial: these still use the built-in TCode v0.3 device
entry too; the BLE block below is the part that actually needs a
custom config because Intiface can't auto-detect a custom BLE
service without it.
FEATURE / RANGE NOTES:
- One linear actuator, command 'L0' (TCode linear axis 0).
- StepCount 1000 -> Intiface scales position 0.0-1.0 to integer 0..999,
matching the firmware's TCODE_MAGNITUDE_MAX (999). e.g. 0.5 -> 'L0500'.
- The web UI's Stroke Window / trim / max-speed math is applied AFTER this
on the device, so Intiface always sends the full 0..999 range and the
machine maps it into your chosen physical window.
- These UUIDs/name MUST stay in sync with include/system/config_api.h:
BLE_DEVICE_NAME        = SlopDrive-32
BLE_NUS_SERVICE_UUID   = 8a846175-ea22-4411-88f5-9a8afcc20671
BLE_NUS_RX_CHAR_UUID   = 8a846175-ea22-4411-88f5-9a8afcc20672 (host -> device, write)
BLE_NUS_TX_CHAR_UUID   = 8a846175-ea22-4411-88f5-9a8afcc20673 (device -> host, notify)
Attach the SlopDrive-32 BLE device to the built-in tcode-v03 protocol.
The Nordic UART Service is used as a transparent serial pipe: Intiface
writes TCode strings to the 'tx' endpoint (the device's RX char) and
reads replies from the 'rx' endpoint (the device's TX/notify char).

websocket.$comment: Optional: lets Intiface treat the ESP32 WebSocket server as a tcode-v03 device by name as well.
